steps.title: Identify the sentence structure at the boundary
steps.content: The sentence has a subject Olympia Brown of ______ that must connect directly to a verb and its object. The blank requires Connecticut, and we must determine what punctuation (if any) belongs between Connecticut and the verb that follows.

steps.title: Apply the rule for subject-verb boundaries
steps.content:
Connecticut played (B) connects the subject directly to its verb with no intervening punctuation, forming one complete independent clause.

A period (A) creates an incorrect sentence boundary, leaving the remaining words as a fragment without a subject.

A semicolon (C) and colon (D) introduce separations designed for independent clauses or introductions, not for joining a subject to its verb.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
